title: RepoPact 1.5.0 Vendor Upgrade and Validator Unification

preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts. Brings ForgeLink's vendored RepoPact from 1.4.0 to 1.5.0, reconciles the evidence drift introduced while closing 015/016/025, fixes audit registry drift, and unifies the two diverging validators so the vendored RepoPact validator is authoritative again.

acceptance_criteria.text: Re-vendor RepoPact 1.5.0 CLI scripts (init_repo, new, takeover, generate_spec, generate_dashboard, doctor, repopact_cli, repo_model) into scripts/, preserving ForgeLink's local preflight extension in validate_repo.py and the schema, and bump the pinned version (scripts/REPOPACT_VERSION, requirements-repopact.txt, audits/registry.json) to 1.5.0.

Reconcile the evidence drift: create the missing formal evidence/runs/*.json records for the criteria closed with dangling evidence ids (CLV-021, CLV-022, AGH-001, AGH-002, AGH-003, AGH-004, and 025) so every satisfied criterion references a real evidence record.
Fix audits/registry.json drift: point the 015 scope at work/completed, register the 015 and 025 nested AGENTS.md contracts, so the vendored validator's contract and audit-scope checks pass.

Unify the validators: .local/validate_system.py runs the vendored RepoPact validate_repo.py as the authoritative governance check and then layers the ForgeLink-only structural checks RepoPact lacks (checkbox parity LIE-001, schema-ladder LIE-003, markdown link/date); the tautological evidence-log check (LIE-002) is retired in favor of RepoPact's formal evidence check. Both validators pass on the tree.

Record a decision documenting the 1.5.0 upgrade, the evidence reconciliation, and the single-authoritative-validator architecture, and note which ForgeLink-local extensions (preflight, checkbox parity, schema-ladder) are candidates to contribute upstream to RepoPact later.
